item.text: I’ll inspect the current directory first so I can add the project files without disturbing anything already here.

The workspace is essentially empty, so I’m going to add the Node project structure from scratch. I’ll keep the module style CommonJS because it keeps the built-in imports and CLI entrypoint straightforward.

Next I’m creating the tracker module, CLI, sample data, README, package metadata, and tests. The module accepts an optional file path for tests while defaulting to the root `requirements.json` for normal CLI use.
The files are in place. I’m going to run the test suite now, then I’ll exercise the demo command so we know both entry points work.
